title: Daily Routines
shortSummary: Describe your day from morning to night, including the separable verbs Germans use constantly.
culturalNote: Daily life in Germany runs on structure. Many people start work early (around 8am), value a proper lunch break, and treasure Feierabend, the cherished moment when the workday ends and free time begins. Sundays are a genuine day of rest (Ruhetag) when shops close and noise rules discourage loud chores, so locals use the day for walks, family, and relaxing.

keyPhrases.german: Ich stehe um sieben Uhr auf
keyPhrases.english: I get up at seven o'clock
keyPhrases.pronunciation: ikh SHTAY-uh oom ZEE-ben oor owf

keyPhrases.english: in the morning / in the evening

grammar.explanation: Many German verbs have a prefix that separates and jumps to the end of the sentence. aufstehen (to get up): 'Ich stehe um sieben auf'. The prefix auf goes to the end.
grammar.commonMistake: Keeping the prefix attached: it is 'Ich stehe auf', not 'Ich aufstehe'.

grammar.explanation: Use 'am' for parts of the day and days of the week: am Morgen, am Abend, am Montag. The exception is 'in der Nacht' (at night).

examples.en: In the morning I drink coffee.

examples.de: Am Freitag gehe ich ins Kino.
examples.en: On Friday I go to the cinema.

grammar.commonMistake: Saying 'in dem Morgen'. Parts of the day take 'am', not 'in dem'.

dialogue.setup: Two friends, Jonas and Mia, compare their daily routines over coffee.

lines.speaker: Jonas
lines.english: When do you usually get up?

lines.speaker: Mia
lines.german: Ich stehe um halb sieben auf. Und du?
lines.english: I get up at half past six. And you?

lines.speaker: Jonas
lines.german: Erst um acht. Ich frühstücke und gehe dann zur Arbeit.
lines.english: Only at eight. I have breakfast and then go to work.

lines.speaker: Mia
lines.english: What do you do in the evening?

lines.speaker: Jonas
lines.german: Ich koche, sehe fern und gehe früh ins Bett.
lines.english: I cook, watch TV, and go to bed early.

lines.speaker: Mia
lines.english: I often go jogging in the evenings.

quiz.question: Ich stehe um sieben Uhr _____. (separable prefix of aufstehen)
quiz.explanation: aufstehen separates and the prefix auf goes to the end.

quiz.question: How do you say 'I have breakfast'?
quiz.explanation: frühstücken is the verb 'to have breakfast'.

quiz.question: Which is correct?
quiz.explanation: fernsehen is separable: sehe second, fern at the end.

quiz.question: How do you say 'in the morning'?
Arrange: gehe / Hause / nach / ich
quiz.explanation: Subject ich, verb gehe, then 'nach Hause' (home).
